channel.title: NPR Topics: Science
channel.description: The latest health and science news. Updates on medicine, healthy living, nutrition, drugs, diet, and advances in science and technology. Subscribe to the Health & Science podcast.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: How Trump's EPA head has transformed the agency — and sided with polluters
item.description: New Yorker writer Elizabeth Kolbert says EPA chief Lee Zeldin has rescinded regulations, cut or eliminated departments and terminated the jobs of many scientists. Trump calls Zeldin "our secret weapon."
item.content:encoded: New Yorker writer Elizabeth Kolbert says EPA chief Lee Zeldin has rescinded regulations, cut or eliminated departments and terminated the jobs of many scientists. Trump calls Zeldin "our secret weapon."
item.dc:creator: Terry Gross

item.title: Like soap operas, 75 feet up: How bald eagle nest cams hook online communities
item.description: Across the country, some 50 bald eagle nests fitted with cameras broadcast up-close views of raptor family life. Every spring, as eggs hatch and eaglets grow, these cameras rake in millions of views.
item.content:encoded: Across the country, some 50 bald eagle nests fitted with cameras broadcast up-close views of raptor family life. Every spring, as eggs hatch and eaglets grow, these cameras rake in millions of views.
item.dc:creator: Ava Berger

item.title: In the midst of an energy crisis, countries make plans to ditch oil, gas and coal
item.description: Against the backdrop of an energy crisis and a warming planet, more than 50 countries have come to Santa Marta, Colombia, to discuss concrete ways to phase out oil, gas and coal.
item.content:encoded:
Against the backdrop of an energy crisis and a warming planet, more than 50 countries have come to Santa Marta, Colombia, to discuss concrete ways to phase out oil, gas and coal.

(Image credit: Raul Arboleda)
item.dc:creator: Julia Simon

item.title: Scientists see Trump's firing of the National Science Board as an attack on research
item.description: The move follows an administration push for cuts to the NSF and raises concerns in the scientific community that it could jeopardize a tradition of independent decisions about federal science grants.
item.content:encoded:
The move follows an administration push for cuts to the NSF and raises concerns in the scientific community that it could jeopardize a tradition of independent decisions about federal science grants.

(Image credit: Mark Schiefelbein)
item.dc:creator: Scott Neuman

item.title: The MAHA movement is mad about the weedkiller glyphosate and Trump's EPA
item.description: The coalition focused on making Americans healthier is frustrated with the Trump administration's stance on environmental toxins and most recently, its support of the company that makes the pesticide.
item.content:encoded: The coalition focused on making Americans healthier is frustrated with the Trump administration's stance on environmental toxins and most recently, its support of the company that makes the pesticide.
item.dc:creator: Will Stone

item.title: Millions of homes in the U.S. are uninsured. NPR wants to hear your story
item.description: Millions of homes in the U.S. are uninsured, partly because insurance costs have soared in recent years. NPR wants to hear about the coverage decisions you're making as premiums rise.
item.content:encoded:
Millions of homes in the U.S. are uninsured, partly because insurance costs have soared in recent years. NPR wants to hear about the coverage decisions you're making as premiums rise.

(Image credit: ROBYN BECK/AFP via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Michael Copley

item.title: Lawsuits accuse State Farm of secretly working to cut insurance payouts
item.description: Lawsuits allege that State Farm tries to avoid paying what it owes for hail damage. The litigation is happening as homeowners face soaring insurance costs, partly due to threats from climate change.
item.content:encoded:
Lawsuits allege that State Farm tries to avoid paying what it owes for hail damage. The litigation is happening as homeowners face soaring insurance costs, partly due to threats from climate change.

(Image credit: Drew Angerer)
item.dc:creator: Michael Copley

item.title: What baby teeth reveal about developing baby brains
item.description: A new study examined baby teeth and found there are critical windows early in a child's life when their developing brains are particularly vulnerable to exposures to metals in the environment.
item.content:encoded: A new study examined baby teeth and found there are critical windows early in a child's life when their developing brains are particularly vulnerable to exposures to metals in the environment.
item.dc:creator: Maria Godoy

item.title: A free diver visited the Strait of Hormuz. Here's what he saw
item.description: The Strait of Hormuz is being blockaded. But a couple weeks ago, one small boat visited and found something surprising below the waves.
item.content:encoded: The Strait of Hormuz is being blockaded. But a couple weeks ago, one small boat visited and found something surprising below the waves.
item.dc:creator: Henry Larson

item.title: A fast-growing Georgia wildfire tops 31 square miles, with evacuations possible
item.description: One of two large wildfires in southeastern Georgia continues to grow and now exceeds 31 square miles.
item.content:encoded:
One of two large wildfires in southeastern Georgia continues to grow and now exceeds 31 square miles.

(Image credit: Office of Gov. Brian Kemp)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
